title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Traditional funds v/s ULIP funds
Overview Of Traditional & ULIP Funds
Life insurance plans can be broadly categorised into two segments – traditional plans and unit linked insurance plans (ULIPs).
Traditional plans include term insurance, endowment plans and money-back plans which do not invest the premium in market-linked securities. They offer stable and secure returns and insurance protection. To understand traditional plan vs ULIP, you need to understand ULIPs also.
ULIPs, on the other hand, are market-linked life insurance plans. They aim to create an investment corpus by investing the premium in different funds. Each of these funds invests in a diversified range of securities available in the financial markets like equity, bonds, money market instruments, etc. As such, under ULIPs, the returns are not guaranteed but dependent on the performance of the funds into which you have invested. Since these funds invest in market-linked securities, ULIP returns are dependent on market movements.
While there are many differences between traditional and ULIP plans, one thing is the same. Both plans provide life insurance coverage during the policy tenure. If the life insured passes away during the policy term, a death benefit is paid to the family to help them meet the financial loss suffered.
Comparison Between Traditional & ULIP Funds
Here is a comparative analysis of traditional plan vs ULIP for a clearer understanding of the two –
However, whether you need to choose a traditional plan or ULIP completely depends on your choice, but you need to weigh the traditional plan vs ULIP before opting for the same.
Advantages Of Traditional Funds
Some of the advantages of traditional life insurance plans are as follows –
Safety and stability
The plan benefits are not subject to market risks and volatility. You get a stable benefit on premature demise or maturity. Even if the market is falling or is volatile, your benefits will remain unaffected.
Bonus adds to the corpus
If you choose to participate in traditional plans, you are entitled to receive a bonus as and when the company declares the same provided the premiums are paid. These bonus additions accrue over the policy tenure and enhance the policy benefits. In some plans, the bonus might also be given out as regular incomes which create an additional source of income for the policyholder.
Useful for long-term financial goals
Traditional life insurance plans come with a diverse range of policy tenure which can go up to 25 or 30 years. There are whole-life plans too wherein the coverage is extended till 99 or 100 years of age. If you have long-term financial goals for which you want to save and create a corpus, you can choose a long-term tenure and build up your savings.
Money-back plans offer liquidity
If you choose money-back life insurance plans, you get regular payouts in the form of money-back benefits. These payouts provide liquidity and you can meet your short-term financial needs.
Now that we understand the advantages of traditional plans, let us also understand the advantages of ULIPs before weighing traditional plan vs ULIPs.
Advantages of ULIP funds
Some of the benefits of ULIPs are as follows –
Insurance and investment
One of the primary benefits of a ULIP is its dual nature. While it provides life insurance coverage over the policy term, it also helps you earn market-linked returns on your investments. Your premiums are invested in market-linked securities through the investment funds that you choose. You can, then, earn returns which are linked to the market and build a good corpus over time.
Flexibility
ULIPs give you complete flexibility in choosing the premium that you want to pay, the fund that you want to invest in, policy tenure, etc. This helps you design your plan per your needs and investment needs. Moreover, if your investment need or strategy changes over time, the switching facility allows you to change your investment funds without incurring any tax liability.
Matches individual investment strategy
ULIPs offer a range of fund options to choose from which include equity, debt and hybrid. You can also allocate your investment across the available funds in any desired proportion. Thus, ULIPs cater to the risk appetite and investment strategy of every individual.
Easy liquidity
ULIPs offer the benefit of partial withdrawals after the lock-in period of 5 years comes to an end. With the partial withdrawal facility, you can access your fund value whenever you need funds for your needs. This makes ULIPs liquid.
Who could buy ULIP?
ULIPs are suitable for individuals who want to enjoy both insurance and investment under a single plan. Individuals looking to earn market-linked returns on their investment along with insurance protection can buy ULIPs. Moreover, there are different types of ULIPs available in the market like child ULIPs, pension ULIPs and endowment ULIPs which can cater to the diverse financial needs that individuals have.
Lastly, ULIPs are also suitable for individuals who want to enjoy the tax benefits that the plan provides, both on investment as well as on the benefits received from the policy.
Who Could Buy Traditional Plans?
Traditional plans are suitable for individuals who want to save for their financial goals but want to avoid any type of market risk or volatility. Individuals looking for goal-specific plans like child plans or needing liquidity from money-back plans can also choose traditional plans for their needs.
Traditional plans are also suitable for long-term investors who want to save for their long-term financial goals in a disciplined manner while enjoying insurance protection over the tenure.
Conclusion
Traditional and ULIPs are two different types of plans, each with its own set of features and benefits. The choice depends on your financial goals, investment horizon, risk appetite and investment strategy. Understand the differences and meanings between ULIP vs traditional ones and then make an informed choice. You can also choose both types of plans for the different types of financial goals that you might have and create a diversified portfolio.
FAQs
Are returns guaranteed in traditional funds, and how do they compare to ULIP Funds?
Under non-participating plans, the returns are usually guaranteed on death or maturity. However, under participating plans, the bonus rate is not guaranteed and so, the returns are not fixed. That being said, traditional plans offer secure and stable returns which are not linked to market performance. There is no negative return from traditional plans.
However, ULIP returns are linked to market performance and are not guaranteed.
What are the charges associated with Traditional Funds and ULIP Funds?
The charges under traditional funds are not disclosed by the insurance company. They are adjusted by the company itself and the policyholder does not know about the same.
Under ULIPs, there are different charges which are deducted from the premium amount. Some of these include premium allocation charges, mortality charges, policy administration charges, fund management charges, etc.
How does liquidity differ between Traditional Funds and ULIP Funds?
Traditional plans usually don’t offer any liquidity. If you need funds, you can either surrender the policy or take a loan against it. Surrendering stops the coverage while loans attract an interest rate. On the other hand, ULIPs offer the facility of partial withdrawals which is available after the lock-in period of 5 years is over. Thus, with ULIPs, you get better liquidity compared to traditional life insurance plans.
